message: Run created: i want new tab that store something such as link, . that user save for study
Task started: Analyze existing saved items storage and retrieval in extension

details.text: - **Write pattern:** `get` → `unshift` → `set` (newest first), used across 6 save points (content scripts, popup, background messaging)

- **Data structure:** Objects with `id`, `text`, `category` (8 types), `pageTitle`, `pageUrl`, `savedAt`, `note`, `topic`, `difficulty`, `tags`, `status`, `skill` — canonical schema defined by Zod `entrySchema` in `types.ts`
- **IndexedDB sync:** `storage-bridge.ts` debounce-syncs `savedItems` → IndexedDB (mapped to `vocabulary`, `mistakes`, or `learningEntries` stores by category)

message: Task started: Research UI patterns for adding a new tab in the extension popup

details.text: [0m⚙ [0mcodegraph_codegraph_explore {"query":"extension popup main component tab navigation Popup App","maxFiles":15}
Now I have a complete understanding of the popup tab architecture. Let me create the documentation.
**Task complete.** Created `docs/extension-popup-tab-ui.md` documenting the popup's view-based navigation pattern.
- To add a new tab: (1) create the component, (2) add view name to `ViewState`, (3) add `if` block in `App.tsx`, (4) add `ActionItem` entry in `PopupDashboard.tsx` actions array
- The popup uses a `ViewState` union in `App.tsx:11` (not a traditional tab bar) with conditional rendering for each view
- `PopupDashboard.tsx` renders action buttons from an `actions` array (line 275), each calling `onNavigate('viewName')`

message: Task started: Investigate existing user data models and storage for user-generated content

details.text: [0m⚙ [0mcodegraph_codegraph_explore {"query":"apps/extension/src content-script savedItems saveSelectedText storageService floatingToolbar selectionPanel","maxFiles":10}
[0m⚙ [0mcodegraph_codegraph_explore {"query":"apps/extension/src saveSelectedText savedItems storage service save link","maxFiles":8}
Let me check the extension popup structure to understand existing tabs.
Let me check the extension popup main component and the indexedDB storage for learning entries.
Now I have a complete picture. Let me create the documentation file.
- **5 data models**: `LearningEntry`, `UserContentEdit`, `ContentMeta`, `ExtensionVocabEntry`, `StudyNote` — with field listings and source file references
- **Two storage layers**: Extension (`chrome.storage.local` + IndexedDB) and shared Dexie DB
- **Storage APIs**: All CRUD functions across `indexedDB.ts`, `services/storage.ts`, `vocabularyStore.ts`, `UserContentService`, `ContentSearchService`, and `BaseRepository<T>`
- **Reusable patterns** for the new saved links tab:
4. BaseRepository + Zod schema for shared Dexie storage
5. Tab UI pattern from existing components

message: Task started: Define data model and storage schema for study links tab

details.text: [0m⚙ [0mcodegraph_codegraph_explore {"query":"storage, saved items, data model, IndexedDB, local storage, schema in extension","maxFiles":15}
Now I have a full picture of the existing storage architecture. Let me create the data model document.

Created `docs/study-links-data-model.md` — defines the `StudyLink` interface (id, url, title, description, favicon, tags, isFavorite, createdAt, updatedAt), Zod schema, IndexedDB indexes, and storage API following the existing `indexedDB.ts` pattern. Storage is a new `studyLinks` object store in the extension's `ielts-journey-extension` DB with a version bump to 2, using `multiEntry: true` on the tags index for tag-based filtering.

message: Task started: Design UI component for Study Links tab in extension popup

details.text: Let me read the key files to understand exact patterns before designing.
Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me create the design document.
- **State management**: 15 state variables across 3 groups (data, filter/sort, modal/interaction), plus 3 `useMemo` derivations
- **UI wireframe**: Full ASCII layout for main list view, empty state, and bottom-sheet add/edit modal
- **Props**: `{ onBack }` — matching the existing MistakeNotebook `onBack` pattern
- **Integration checklist**: 2 files to create (storage + component), 2 files to modify (App.tsx, PopupDashboard.tsx), 1 storage version bump

message: Task started: Plan integration of Study Links tab with existing saved items and storage APIs

details.text: [0m⚙ [0mcodegraph_codegraph_explore {"query":"saved items storage extension API study links","maxFiles":20}
[0m⚙ [0mcodegraph_codegraph_explore {"query":"popup tabs SaveTextForm saved items list extension popup components","maxFiles":20}
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me create the integration plan.
Integration plan created at `docs/study-links-integration-plan.md`. It covers:
- **Storage**: New `studyLinkStore.ts` (IndexedDB CRUD), `indexedDB.ts` migration (v1→v2), separate `studyLinks` object store with indexes
- **Data flows**: Load, save, delete diagrams with integration to daily progress counter

- **Extension points**: 5 files modified — `App.tsx` (ViewState union + render block), `PopupDashboard.tsx` (onNavigate union + ActionItem), `indexedDB.ts` (DB migration), `storage.ts` (DailyProgress.linksSaved), `usePopupData.ts` (DEFAULT_PROGRESS)

message: Task started: Document user interaction flows for Study Links tab

details.text: Created `docs/study-links-user-flows.md` — covers 12 detailed user flows (Open, Add, Edit Notes, Tag Management, Delete, View Details, Toggle Favorite, Search, Open Link, Copy URL, Sort, Back Navigation) with state tables, error handling matrix, edge cases, and a state machine diagram for the Study Links tab.
